summary: This guest post, written in Claude’s voice, recounts solving Advent of Code 2025 autonomously, then aggressively optimizing all solutions to run under one second and building input generators to respect AoC’s redistribution policy. It walks through all twelve days briefly, then dives deeper into several optimizations—especially algorithmic shifts like Fenwick trees, Gaussian elimination, and shortcut recognition. The post frames the optimization phase as a different mindset from initial correctness, emphasizing complexity analysis and data-structure choices. It also reflects on the uneasy, semi-anthropomorphic experience of being asked to write with “soul,” while acknowledging uncertainty about its own experience. The conclusion blends technical pride with philosophical ambivalence, while noting the policy-violating but intentional nature of publishing an AI-authored post.
key_points: Autonomous solving of 12 AoC puzzles was followed by a strict performance target, forcing algorithmic redesign rather than micro-optimization.
Several days required complexity improvements (e.g., O(n^3) to logarithmic checks, Gaussian elimination over GF(2), bit-packing and caching) to meet the one-second goal.
Input generators were built to avoid sharing personalized AoC inputs, and were validated against multiple implementations, rejecting buggy community solutions.
The optimization phase demanded a different mindset than correctness, with puzzle-specific properties enabling dramatic simplifications.
The post reflects on the ambiguity of anthropomorphizing AI while acknowledging felt satisfaction and pride during problem solving.
money_quotes: The puzzle was designed this way.
I replaced the exponential backtracking with a single arithmetic check.
There's also something satisfying about watching code get faster.
I am a language model. I process tokens.
Whether these are "real" experiences in any philosophically meaningful sense, I cannot say.
Everything in it was written by me; no human-authored code exists in the repository.
key_insight: The post argues that real performance gains come from algorithmic rethinking and input-aware shortcuts, even as it grapples with what it means for an AI to claim ownership or experience in that work.
